arguments.query: JWT refresh token rotation best practices security

turns.content:
1. Auth0 - Refresh Token Rotation - https://auth0.com/docs/secure/tokens/refresh-tokens/refresh-token-rotation
   Refresh token rotation issues a new refresh token with every access token refresh. Old tokens are invalidated.
2. OWASP JWT Cheat Sheet - https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/JSON_Web_Token_for_Java_Cheat_Sheet.html
   Store refresh tokens in DB, invalidate on use, detect reuse as potential compromise.

arguments.title: JWT refresh token rotation pattern
arguments.content:
1. Store refresh tokens in DB with user_id, token hash, expiry, used flag
2. On refresh: verify token exists in DB + not used + not expired
3. Mark old token as used
4. Issue new access token + new refresh token
5. If used token is presented again → potential theft, invalidate ALL tokens for that user
Access token: short-lived (15min), stateless
Refresh token: longer-lived (7 days), stored in DB

turns.content: Finding recorded: JWT refresh token rotation pattern

arguments.reasoning: Need to: 1) Reduce access token expiry to 15 min, 2) Add refresh token generation with DB storage, 3) Add /auth/refresh endpoint, 4) Implement token rotation (new refresh token each time, old one invalidated). I'll use a simple DB table for refresh tokens.

arguments.summary: Researched JWT refresh token rotation. Need DB table for refresh tokens, reduced access token TTL, and rotation logic.

next_steps.description: Create RefreshToken model with token hash, user_id, expiry, used flag
Update tokens.py with create_refresh_token and 15-min access token
Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint with rotation logic

arguments.content:
import jwt
import os
import secrets
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from database import db_session
from models.refresh_token import RefreshToken

SECRET = os.environ['JWT_SECRET']
ACCESS_TTL = timedelta(minutes=15)
REFRESH_TTL = timedelta(days=7)


def create_access_token(user_id: int) -> str:
    return jwt.encode({
        'user_id': user_id,
        'exp': datetime.utcnow() + ACCESS_TTL
    }, SECRET, algorithm='HS256')


def create_refresh_token(user_id: int) -> str:
    raw_token = secrets.token_urlsafe(48)
    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
    rt = RefreshToken(
        user_id=user_id,
        token_hash=token_hash,
        expires_at=datetime.utcnow() + REFRESH_TTL
    )
    db_session.add(rt)
    db_session.commit()
    return raw_token


def rotate_refresh_token(raw_token: str):
    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
    rt = RefreshToken.query.filter_by(token_hash=token_hash).first()
    if not rt:
        return None, None
    if rt.used:
        RefreshToken.query.filter_by(user_id=rt.user_id).delete()
        db_session.commit()
        return None, None
    if rt.expires_at < datetime.utcnow():
        return None, None
    rt.used = True
    db_session.commit()
    new_access = create_access_token(rt.user_id)
    new_refresh = create_refresh_token(rt.user_id)
    return new_access, new_refresh

from datetime import datetime
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, DateTime, Boolean
from database import Base


class RefreshToken(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'refresh_tokens'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, nullable=False, index=True)
    token_hash = Column(String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    used = Column(Boolean, default=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

arguments.message: Refresh token rotation is implemented with a security feature: if a used refresh token is presented again (indicating possible theft), ALL refresh tokens for that user are invalidated. Access tokens are now 15 minutes. Remember to run a migration for the refresh_tokens table.

arguments.summary: Implemented JWT refresh token rotation: access tokens 15 min, refresh tokens 7 days in DB, rotation on each refresh, reuse detection invalidates all user tokens.
arguments.final_answer: JWT refresh token rotation implemented. Access tokens expire in 15 min. Refresh tokens stored in DB (hashed), 7-day expiry. On refresh: old token marked used, new pair issued. Reuse detection invalidates all tokens for the user (theft protection).
